chapter_context.summary: Jehoiada the priest leads a coup, crowns seven-year-old Joash, executes Athaliah, destroys Baal's house, and restores covenant worship.

outline.text: Jehoiada's Covenant with Captains; Crowns Joash King of Judah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehoiada's Covenant with Captains; Crowns Joash King of Judah (23:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Athaliah Cries 'Treason!' and is Executed Outside
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Athaliah Cries 'Treason!' and is Executed Outside (23:12-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Jehoiada Renews Covenants; Priest of Baal Slain; Joash Reigns
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehoiada Renews Covenants; Priest of Baal Slain; Joash Reigns (23:16-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: The Temple Centered Kingdom
key_themes.description: The presence of God's glory in the Temple is the absolute center of national blessing and life.

key_themes.title: Immediate Retribution and Reform
key_themes.description: Good kings who seek the LORD experience immediate victory and peace, while unfaithfulness leads to immediate warning and exile.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 23 prompt you to humble yourself, seek God's face, and turn away from compromise?
What practical reforms can you make in your daily walk to ensure your heart is dedicated to God?

cultural_historical.title: The Levitical Worship System
cultural_historical.text: Unlike the Book of Kings, Chronicles highlights the roles of singers, Levites, and liturgical musicians in the temple's daily administration.
